docs.title: python-re3data: The pythonic client for the re3data API.
Requirements
docs.text:
Python >= 3.10

python-re3data is built with:

  • httpx for issuing HTTP requests

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
You can install python-re3data via pip from PyPI:

python -m pip install python-re3data

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text: The documentation is made with Material for MkDocs and is hosted by GitHub Pages.

docs.title: License
docs.text: python-re3data is distributed under the terms of the MIT License.

docs.title: Contributing to python-re3data
docs.text:
Contributions are welcome, and they are greatly appreciated! Every little bit helps, and credit will always be given.

You can contribute in many ways.

docs.title: Report Bugs
docs.text:
Report bugs at https://github.com/afuetterer/python-re3data/issues.

If you are reporting a bug, please include:

  • Your operating system name and version.
  • Any details about your local setup that might be helpful in troubleshooting.
  • Detailed steps to reproduce the bug.

docs.title: Fix Bugs
docs.text: Look through the GitHub issues for bugs. Anything tagged with bug and help wanted is open to whoever wants to implement it.

docs.title: Implement Features
docs.text: Look through the GitHub issues for features. Anything tagged with feature and help wanted is open to whoever wants to implement it.

docs.title: Write Documentation
docs.text: python-re3data could always use more documentation, whether as part of the official python-re3data docs, in docstrings, or even on the web in blog posts, articles, and such.

docs.title: Submit Feedback
docs.text:
The best way to send feedback is to file an issue at https://github.com/afuetterer/python-re3data/issues.

If you are proposing a feature:

  • Explain in detail how it would work.
  • Keep the scope as narrow as possible, to make it easier to implement.
  • Remember that this is a volunteer-driven project, and that contributions are welcome.

docs.title: Get Started!
docs.text:
Ready to contribute?

You need Python 3.10+ and hatch. You can install it globally with pipx:

$ pipx install hatch

or locally with (this will install it in the local virtual environment):

$ pip install hatch

Here's how to set up python-re3data for local development.

  1. Fork the python-re3data repository on GitHub.
  2. Clone your fork locally: 
    $ git clone git@github.com:username/python-re3data.git
  3. Install your local copy into a virtual environment. Assuming you have hatch installed, this is how you set up your fork for local development: 
    $ cd python-re3data
$ hatch shell
$ pre-commit install

  4. Create a branch for local development: 

    $ git checkout -b name-of-your-bugfix-or-feature
    Now you can make your changes locally.

  5. When you're done making changes, check that your changes pass pre-commit and the tests: 

    $ hatch run lint:all
$ hatch run cov

  6. Commit your changes and push your branch to GitHub:: 

    $ git add .
$ git commit -m "Your detailed description of your changes."
$ git push origin name-of-your-bugfix-or-feature

  7. Submit a pull request through the GitHub website.

docs.title: Pull Request Guidelines
docs.text:
Before you submit a pull request, check that it meets these guidelines:

  1. The pull request should include tests.
  2. If the pull request adds functionality, the docs should be updated. Put your new functionality into a function with a docstring.
  3. The pull request should work for Python >= 3.10. Check https://github.com/afuetterer/python-re3data/pulls and make sure that all the tests pass.

This contributor guide is adapted from cookiecutter-pypackage (BSD 3-Clause License).
